@sl-slua/global/assert.documentation: Checks if the value is truthy; if not, raises an error with the optional message.

@sl-slua/global/dangerouslyexecuterequir.documentation: Dangerously executes a required module function

@sl-slua/global/error.documentation: Raises an error with the specified object and optional call stack level.

@sl-slua/global/gcinfo.documentation: Returns the total heap size in kilobytes.

@sl-slua/global/getmetatable.documentation: Returns the metatable for the specified object.

@sl-slua/global/ipairs.documentation: Returns an iterator for numeric key-value pairs in the table.

@sl-slua/global/newproxy.documentation: Creates a new untyped userdata object with an optional metatable.

@sl-slua/global/next.documentation: Returns the next key-value pair in the table traversal order.

@sl-slua/global/pairs.documentation: Returns an iterator for all key-value pairs in the table.

@sl-slua/global/pcall.documentation: Calls function f with parameters args, returning success and function results or an error.

@sl-slua/global/print.documentation: Prints all arguments to standard output using Tab as a separator.

@sl-slua/global/rawequal.documentation: Returns true if a and b have the same type and value.

@sl-slua/global/rawget.documentation: Performs a table lookup bypassing metatables.

@sl-slua/global/rawlen.documentation: Returns the length of a table or string bypassing metatables.

@sl-slua/global/rawset.documentation: Assigns a value to a table field bypassing metatables.

@sl-slua/global/select.documentation: Returns a subset of arguments or the number of arguments.

@sl-slua/global/setmetatable.documentation: Changes the metatable for the given table.

@sl-slua/global/tonumber.documentation: Converts the input string to a number in the specified base.

@sl-slua/global/toquaternion.documentation: Converts a string to a quaternion, returns nil if invalid

@sl-slua/global/torotation.documentation: Converts a string to a rotation (quaternion), returns nil if invalid

@sl-slua/global/tostring.documentation: Converts the input object to a string.

@sl-slua/global/touuid.documentation: Creates a new uuid from a string, buffer, or existing uuid. Returns nil if the string is not a valid UUID, or the buffer is shorter than 16 bytes.

@sl-slua/global/tovector.documentation: Converts a string to a vector, returns nil if invalid

@sl-slua/global/type.documentation: Returns the type of the object as a string.

@sl-slua/global/typeof.documentation: Returns the type of the object, including custom userdata types.

@sl-slua/global/unpack.documentation: Returns values from an array in the specified index range.

@sl-slua/global/xpcall.documentation: Calls function f with parameters args, handling errors with err if they occur.

@sl-slua/global/bit32.arshift.documentation:
Shifts n by i bits to the right. If i is negative, a left shift is performed.
Does an arithmetic shift: The most significant bit of n is propagated during the shift.

@sl-slua/global/bit32.band.documentation: Performs a bitwise AND operation on input numbers.

@sl-slua/global/bit32.bnot.documentation: Returns the bitwise negation of the input number.

@sl-slua/global/bit32.bor.documentation: Performs a bitwise OR operation on input numbers.

@sl-slua/global/bit32.bxor.documentation: Performs a bitwise XOR operation on input numbers.

@sl-slua/global/bit32.btest.documentation:
Performs a bitwise AND operation on input numbers.
Returns true if result is non-zero.

@sl-slua/global/bit32.extract.documentation: Extracts bits from n at position field with width

@sl-slua/global/bit32.lrotate.documentation: Rotates n by i bits to the left. If i is negative, a right rotate is performed.

@sl-slua/global/bit32.lshift.documentation: Shifts n by i bits to the left. If i is negative, a right shift is performed.

@sl-slua/global/bit32.replace.documentation: Replaces bits in n at position field with width using value v

@sl-slua/global/bit32.rrotate.documentation: Rotates n by i bits to the right. If i is negative, a left rotate is performed.

@sl-slua/global/bit32.rshift.documentation: Shifts n by i bits to the right. If i is negative, a left shift is performed.

@sl-slua/global/bit32.s32.documentation: Wrap this number from float64 range to signed int32 range and truncate to integer. Makes integer arithmetic compatible with LSL.

@sl-slua/global/bit32.smul.documentation: Multiplies two signed 32-bit integers. Returns the result as a signed 32-bit integer, wrapping as necessary. Avoids precision loss associated with float64 multiplication. Compatible with LSL integer multiplication.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.documentation: Buffer manipulation library for binary data.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.create.documentation: Creates a buffer of the requested size with all bytes initialized to 0.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.fromstring.documentation: Creates a buffer initialized to the contents of the string.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.tostring.documentation: Returns the buffer data as a string.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.readi8.documentation: Reads a signed 8-bit integer from the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.readu8.documentation: Reads an unsigned 8-bit integer from the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.readi16.documentation: Reads a signed 16-bit integer from the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.readu16.documentation: Reads an unsigned 16-bit integer from the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.readi32.documentation: Reads a signed 32-bit integer from the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.readu32.documentation: Reads an unsigned 32-bit integer from the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.readf32.documentation: Reads a 32-bit floating-point number from the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.readf64.documentation: Reads a 64-bit floating-point number from the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.writei8.documentation: Writes a signed 8-bit integer to the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.writeu8.documentation: Writes an unsigned 8-bit integer to the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.writei16.documentation: Writes a signed 16-bit integer to the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.writeu16.documentation: Writes an unsigned 16-bit integer to the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.writei32.documentation: Writes a signed 32-bit integer to the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.writeu32.documentation: Writes an unsigned 32-bit integer to the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.writef32.documentation: Writes a 32-bit floating-point number to the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.writef64.documentation: Writes a 64-bit floating-point number to the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.readstring.documentation: Reads a string of the given length from the buffer at the specified offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.writestring.documentation: Writes data from a string into the buffer at the specified offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.len.documentation: Returns the size of the buffer in bytes.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.copy.documentation: Copies bytes from the source buffer into the target buffer.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.fill.documentation: Fills the buffer with the specified value starting at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.readbits.documentation: Reads up to 32 bits from the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/buffer.writebits.documentation: Writes up to 32 bits to the buffer at the given offset.

@sl-slua/global/coroutine.create.documentation: Returns a new coroutine that, when resumed, will run function f.

@sl-slua/global/coroutine.resume.documentation: Resumes a coroutine, returning true and results if successful, or false and an error.

@sl-slua/global/coroutine.running.documentation: Returns the currently running coroutine, or nil if called from the main coroutine.

@sl-slua/global/coroutine.status.documentation: Returns the status of the coroutine: "running", "suspended", "normal", or "dead".

@sl-slua/global/coroutine.wrap.documentation: Creates a coroutine and returns a function that resumes it.

@sl-slua/global/coroutine.yield.documentation: Yields the current coroutine, passing arguments to the resuming code.

@sl-slua/global/coroutine.isyieldable.documentation: Returns true if the currently running coroutine can yield.

@sl-slua/global/coroutine.close.documentation: Closes a coroutine, returning true if successful or false and an error.

@sl-slua/global/debug.info.documentation: Returns information about a stack frame or function based on specified format.

@sl-slua/global/debug.traceback.documentation: Returns a human-readable call stack starting from the specified level.

@sl-slua/global/llbase64.encode.documentation: Encodes a string or buffer to base64

@sl-slua/global/llbase64.decode.documentation: Decodes a base64 string to a string, or buffer if asBuffer is true. The output is truncated at the first decoding error.

@sl-slua/global/llhttp.request.documentation: Send an HTTP request. Returns a key identifying the request, which is delivered via the http_response event. Omit body for requests that do not need a body (e.g. GET).

@sl-slua/global/lljson.documentation: JSON encoding/decoding library for Second Life.

@sl-slua/global/lljson.null.documentation: A constant to pass for null to json encode.

@sl-slua/global/lljson.remove.documentation: A constant to return from a reviver/replacer replacer function to omit this item.

@sl-slua/global/lljson.array_mt.documentation:
Value: {__jsonhint = "array"}
Metatable for declaring table as an array for json encode.

@sl-slua/global/lljson.object_mt.documentation:
Value: {__jsonhint = "object"}
Metatable for declaring table as an object for json encode.

@sl-slua/global/lljson.empty_array.documentation:
Value: setmetatable({}, lljson.array_mt)
A constant to pass for an empty array to json encode.

@sl-slua/global/lljson.empty_object.documentation:
Value: setmetatable({}, lljson.object_mt)
A constant to pass for an empty object to json encode.

@sl-slua/global/lljson.encode.documentation: Encodes a Lua value as JSON. Raises an error if value contains unsupported types.

@sl-slua/global/lljson.decode.documentation: Decodes a JSON string to a Lua value. Raises an error if JSON is invalid.

@sl-slua/global/lljson.slencode.documentation: Encodes a Lua value as JSON, preserving SL types. Use tight to encode more compactly. Raises an error if value contains unsupported types.

@sl-slua/global/lljson.sldecode.documentation: Decodes a JSON string to a Lua value, preserving SL types. Raises an error if JSON is invalid.

@sl-slua/global/llprim.documentation: Utilities for working with prims / objects

@sl-slua/global/llprim.ParamsSetter.documentation:
Value: PrimParamsSetterTypeMeta
Metatable for building lists to pass to ll.SetLinkPrimitiveParamsFast

@sl-slua/global/llprim.particleSystem.documentation: Emit a particle system from a ParticleParams table. Omit link to apply to the current prim.

@sl-slua/global/llprim.setMedia.documentation: Configure media on a specific face. Omit link to apply to the current prim.

@sl-slua/global/math.huge.documentation:
Value: inf
A value larger than any other numeric value (infinity)

@sl-slua/global/math.abs.documentation: Returns the absolute value of n.

@sl-slua/global/math.acos.documentation: Returns the arc cosine of n in radians.

@sl-slua/global/math.asin.documentation: Returns the arc sine of n in radians.

@sl-slua/global/math.atan.documentation: Returns the arc tangent of n in radians.

@sl-slua/global/math.atan2.documentation: Returns the arc tangent of y/x in radians, using the signs to determine the quadrant.

@sl-slua/global/math.ceil.documentation: Returns the smallest integer larger than or equal to n.

@sl-slua/global/math.clamp.documentation: Returns n clamped between min and max.

@sl-slua/global/math.cos.documentation: Returns the cosine of n (n is in radians).

@sl-slua/global/math.cosh.documentation: Returns the hyperbolic cosine of n.

@sl-slua/global/math.deg.documentation: Converts n from radians to degrees.

@sl-slua/global/math.exp.documentation: Returns the base-e exponent of n.

@sl-slua/global/math.floor.documentation: Returns the largest integer smaller than or equal to n.

@sl-slua/global/math.fmod.documentation: Returns the remainder of x modulo y, rounded towards zero.

@sl-slua/global/math.frexp.documentation: Returns m and e such that n = m * 2^e.

@sl-slua/global/math.lerp.documentation: Linearly interpolates between a and b using factor t.

@sl-slua/global/math.log.documentation: Returns the logarithm of n in the given base (default e).

@sl-slua/global/math.log10.documentation: Returns the base-10 logarithm of n.

@sl-slua/global/math.map.documentation: Maps n from input range to output range.

@sl-slua/global/math.max.documentation: Returns the maximum value from the given numbers.

@sl-slua/global/math.min.documentation: Returns the minimum value from the given numbers.

@sl-slua/global/math.modf.documentation: Returns the integer and fractional parts of n.

@sl-slua/global/math.noise.documentation: Returns Perlin noise value for the point (x, y, z).

@sl-slua/global/math.pow.documentation: Returns base to the power of exponent.

@sl-slua/global/math.rad.documentation: Converts n from degrees to radians.

@sl-slua/global/math.random.documentation: Returns a random number within the given range.

@sl-slua/global/math.randomseed.documentation: Sets the seed for the random number generator.

@sl-slua/global/math.round.documentation: Rounds n to the nearest integer.

@sl-slua/global/math.sign.documentation: Returns -1 if n is negative, 1 if positive, and 0 if zero.

@sl-slua/global/math.sin.documentation: Returns the sine of n (n is in radians).

@sl-slua/global/math.sinh.documentation: Returns the hyperbolic sine of n.

@sl-slua/global/math.sqrt.documentation: Returns the square root of n.

@sl-slua/global/math.tan.documentation: Returns the tangent of n (n is in radians).

@sl-slua/global/math.tanh.documentation: Returns the hyperbolic tangent of n.

@sl-slua/global/math.isnan.documentation: Returns true if n is NaN.

@sl-slua/global/math.isinf.documentation: Returns true if n is infinite.

@sl-slua/global/math.isfinite.documentation: Returns true if n is finite.

@sl-slua/global/os.clock.documentation: Returns a high-precision timestamp in seconds for measuring durations. Has a resolution of 0.0001s (222 ticks per server frame, 3 ticks per script time slice). 6-11x slower to look up than ll.GetTime.

@sl-slua/global/os.date.documentation: Returns a table or string representation of the time based on the provided format.

@sl-slua/global/os.difftime.documentation: Returns the difference in seconds between two timestamps. Same as a - b.

@sl-slua/global/os.time.documentation: Returns the current Unix timestamp or the timestamp of the given date.

@sl-slua/global/quaternion.documentation: Creates a new quaternion with the given component values. Alias of quaternion.create.

@sl-slua/global/quaternion.create.documentation: Creates a new quaternion with the given component values.

@sl-slua/global/quaternion.normalize.documentation: Computes the normalized version (unit quaternion) of the quaternion.

@sl-slua/global/quaternion.magnitude.documentation: Computes the magnitude of the quaternion.

@sl-slua/global/quaternion.dot.documentation: Computes the dot product of two quaternions.

@sl-slua/global/quaternion.slerp.documentation: Spherical linear interpolation from a to b using factor t.

@sl-slua/global/quaternion.conjugate.documentation: Computes the conjugate of the quaternion.

@sl-slua/global/quaternion.tofwd.documentation: Computes the forward vector from the quaternion.

@sl-slua/global/quaternion.toleft.documentation: Computes the left vector from the quaternion.

@sl-slua/global/quaternion.toup.documentation: Computes the up vector from the quaternion.

@sl-slua/global/rotation.create.documentation: Creates a new quaternion with the given component values.

@sl-slua/global/rotation.normalize.documentation: Computes the normalized version (unit quaternion) of the quaternion.

@sl-slua/global/rotation.magnitude.documentation: Computes the magnitude of the quaternion.

@sl-slua/global/rotation.dot.documentation: Computes the dot product of two quaternions.

@sl-slua/global/rotation.slerp.documentation: Spherical linear interpolation from a to b using factor t.

@sl-slua/global/rotation.conjugate.documentation: Computes the conjugate of the quaternion.

@sl-slua/global/rotation.tofwd.documentation: Computes the forward vector from the quaternion.

@sl-slua/global/rotation.toleft.documentation: Computes the left vector from the quaternion.

@sl-slua/global/rotation.toup.documentation: Computes the up vector from the quaternion.

@sl-slua/global/string.byte.documentation: Returns the numeric code of every byte in the input string within the given range.

@sl-slua/global/string.char.documentation: Returns a string containing characters for the given byte values.

@sl-slua/global/string.find.documentation: Finds the first instance of the pattern in the string.

@sl-slua/global/string.format.documentation: Formats input values into a string using printf-style format specifiers.

@sl-slua/global/string.gmatch.documentation: Returns an iterator function for pattern matches

@sl-slua/global/string.gsub.documentation: Performs pattern-based substitution in a string.

@sl-slua/global/string.len.documentation: Returns the number of bytes in the string. Identical to #s

@sl-slua/global/string.lower.documentation: Returns a lowercase version of the input string. Only works with ASCII. Use ll.ToLower for strings that may contain Unicode.

@sl-slua/global/string.match.documentation: Finds and returns matches for a pattern in the input string.

@sl-slua/global/string.pack.documentation: Packs values into a binary string.

@sl-slua/global/string.packsize.documentation: Returns the size of a packed string for the given format.

@sl-slua/global/string.rep.documentation: Returns the input string repeated a given number of times.

@sl-slua/global/string.reverse.documentation: Returns the input string with bytes in reverse order.

@sl-slua/global/string.split.documentation: Splits a string by separator. Returns a list of substrings.

@sl-slua/global/string.sub.documentation: Returns a substring from the given range.

@sl-slua/global/string.unpack.documentation: Decodes a binary string using a pack format.

@sl-slua/global/string.upper.documentation: Returns an uppercase version of the input string. Only works with ASCII. Use ll.ToUpper for strings that may contain Unicode.

@sl-slua/global/table.documentation: Table manipulation library. Tables are collections of key-value pairs.

@sl-slua/global/table.concat.documentation: Joins an array of strings into one string, with an optional separator.

@sl-slua/global/table.foreach.documentation: Iterates over all key-value pairs in the table (deprecated).

@sl-slua/global/table.foreachi.documentation: Iterates over all index-value pairs in the array (deprecated).

@sl-slua/global/table.getn.documentation: Returns the length of an array (deprecated; use # instead).

@sl-slua/global/table.maxn.documentation: Returns the highest numeric key in the table.

@sl-slua/global/table.insert.documentation: Inserts an element at the specified index, or at the end of the array.

@sl-slua/global/table.append.documentation: Appends one or more an elements to end of the array.

@sl-slua/global/table.extend.documentation: Appends all elements from one array to the end of another. Shorthand for table.move(b, 1, #b, #a+1, a)

@sl-slua/global/table.remove.documentation: Removes and returns the element at the specified index from the array, or from the end of the array.

@sl-slua/global/table.pack.documentation: Packs multiple arguments into a new array with length field n.

@sl-slua/global/table.unpack.documentation: Unpacks array elements into multiple return values.

@sl-slua/global/table.move.documentation: Inserts elements [i..j] from src array into dest array at [d].

@sl-slua/global/table.create.documentation: Creates a new table with pre-allocated array capacity, optionally filled.

@sl-slua/global/table.find.documentation: Finds the first occurrence of a value in the array and returns its index.

@sl-slua/global/table.clear.documentation: Clears all elements from a table while keeping its capacity.

@sl-slua/global/table.shrink.documentation: Reduces the memory usage of the table to the minimum necessary.

@sl-slua/global/table.freeze.documentation: Freezes a table, making it read-only.

@sl-slua/global/table.isfrozen.documentation: Returns true if a table is frozen.

@sl-slua/global/table.clone.documentation: Creates a shallow copy of the table.

@sl-slua/global/utf8.charpattern.documentation:
Value: "[\x00-\x7F\xC2-\xF4][\x80-\xBF]*"
Pattern that matches exactly one UTF-8 byte sequence

@sl-slua/global/utf8.char.documentation: Creates a string from Unicode codepoints.

@sl-slua/global/utf8.codes.documentation: Returns an iterator that produces the byte offset and Unicode codepoint for each character in the string.

@sl-slua/global/utf8.codepoint.documentation: Returns the Unicode codepoints in the specified range of the string.

@sl-slua/global/utf8.len.documentation: Returns the number of Unicode codepoints in the specified range of the string, or nil and error index.

@sl-slua/global/utf8.offset.documentation: Returns the byte offset of the nth Unicode codepoint in the string.

@sl-slua/global/uuid.documentation: Creates a new uuid from a string, buffer, or existing uuid. Throws an error if the string is not a valid UUID, or the buffer is shorter than 16 bytes. Alias of uuid.create.

@sl-slua/global/uuid.create.documentation: Creates a new uuid from a string, buffer, or existing uuid. Throws an error if the string is not a valid UUID, or the buffer is shorter than 16 bytes.

@sl-slua/global/vector.documentation: Creates a new vector with the given component values. Alias of vector.create.

@sl-slua/global/vector.zero.documentation:
Value: vector(0, 0, 0)
Constant vector with all components set to 0.

@sl-slua/global/vector.one.documentation:
Value: vector(1, 1, 1)
Constant vector with all components set to 1.

@sl-slua/global/vector.create.documentation: Creates a new vector with the given component values.

@sl-slua/global/vector.magnitude.documentation: Computes the magnitude of the vector.

@sl-slua/global/vector.normalize.documentation: Computes the normalized version (unit vector) of the vector.

@sl-slua/global/vector.cross.documentation: Computes the cross product of two vectors.

@sl-slua/global/vector.dot.documentation: Computes the dot product of two vectors.

@sl-slua/global/vector.angle.documentation: Computes the angle between two vectors in radians. The axis, if specified, is used to determine the sign of the angle.

@sl-slua/global/vector.floor.documentation: Applies math.floor to each component of the vector.

@sl-slua/global/vector.ceil.documentation: Applies math.ceil to each component of the vector.

@sl-slua/global/vector.abs.documentation: Applies math.abs to each component of the vector.

@sl-slua/global/vector.sign.documentation: Applies math.sign to each component of the vector.

@sl-slua/global/vector.clamp.documentation: Clamps each component of the vector between min and max values.

@sl-slua/global/vector.max.documentation: Applies math.max to each component of the vectors.

@sl-slua/global/vector.min.documentation: Applies math.min to each component of the vectors.

@sl-slua/global/vector.lerp.documentation: Linearly interpolates between a and b using factor t.

@sl-slua/global/ll.documentation: Library for functions shared between LSL and SLua.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Abs.documentation: Returns an integer representing the absolute (positive) value of val.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Acos.documentation: Returns a float representing the arc-cosine of val in radians.

@sl-slua/global/ll.AddToLandBanList.documentation: Adds the avatar to the parcel ban list for the specified number of hours. A value of 0 hours adds the avatar indefinitely. Banned users teleporting to the parcel are redirected to a neighboring parcel; the minimum accepted duration is 0.01 hours (approximately 36 seconds).

@sl-slua/global/ll.AddToLandPassList.documentation: Adds the avatar to the land pass list for the specified number of hours (or indefinitely if hours is 0).

@sl-slua/global/ll.AdjustDamage.documentation: Modifies the amount of damage applied by the current on_damage event after it completes processing, specified by the damage event index number.

@sl-slua/global/ll.AdjustSoundVolume.documentation: Adjusts the volume of the currently playing attached sound (has no effect on sounds started with llTriggerSound).

@sl-slua/global/ll.AgentInExperience.documentation: Returns TRUE if the specified agent is in the experience and the experience can run in the current region/location; returns FALSE otherwise.

@sl-slua/global/ll.AllowInventoryDrop.documentation: If add is TRUE, allows users without object modify permissions to drop inventory items into the prim. If FALSE, restricts inventory dropping to users with modify permissions.

@sl-slua/global/ll.AngleBetween.documentation: Returns a float representing the angle in radians between rotations a and b.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ApplyImpulse.documentation: Applies a linear impulse (momentum) to a physical object. If local is TRUE, the impulse is applied in local coordinates; otherwise, it is applied in global region coordinates.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ApplyRotationalImpuls.documentation: Applies a rotational impulse (force) to a physical object. If local is TRUE, the rotational impulse is applied in local coordinates; otherwise, it is applied in global region coordinates.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Asin.documentation: Returns a float representing the arc-sine of val in radians.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Atan2.documentation: Returns a float representing the arctangent2 of y and x.

@sl-slua/global/ll.AttachToAvatar.documentation: Attaches the object to the avatar who has granted the PERMISSION_ATTACH permission. Takes the object into the user's inventory and attaches it at attach_point.

@sl-slua/global/ll.AttachToAvatarTemp.documentation: Attaches the object temporarily to an avatar who has granted the PERMISSION_ATTACH permission. No permanent inventory is created, and the object disappears on detach or disconnect. Can be used on non-owners (changing ownership to the wearer).

@sl-slua/global/ll.AvatarOnLinkSitTarget.documentation: Returns the UUID of the avatar seated on the specified link's sit target, or NULL_KEY if no avatar is sitting there.

@sl-slua/global/ll.AvatarOnSitTarget.documentation: Returns the UUID of the avatar seated on the prim's sit target (defined via llSitTarget), or NULL_KEY if no avatar is sitting there or the prim lacks a sit target.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Axes2Rot.documentation: Returns the rotation defined by the coordinate axes fwd, left, and up.

@sl-slua/global/ll.AxisAngle2Rot.documentation: Returns the rotation representing a generated angle (in radians) about the specified axis.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Base64ToInteger.documentation: Returns an integer representing the Base64-decoded big-endian value of str. Returns zero if str is longer than 8 characters; the return value is unpredictable if str contains fewer than 6 characters.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Base64ToString.documentation: Decodes the Base64-encoded string str into a conventional string, interpreting the bytes as a UTF-8 character sequence. Unprintable characters are converted to question marks.

@sl-slua/global/ll.BreakAllLinks.documentation: Delinks all prims in the linkset. Requires the PERMISSION_CHANGE_LINKS runtime permission, which must be requested and granted by the owner.

@sl-slua/global/ll.BreakLink.documentation: Delinks the prim specified by the link number link. Requires the PERMISSION_CHANGE_LINKS runtime permission.

@sl-slua/global/ll.CSV2List.documentation: Parses the comma-separated string src and returns it as a list.

@sl-slua/global/ll.CastRay.documentation: Casts a ray into the physics world from start to end and reports collision data for intersections with objects based on options. Returns a list of strided values [UUID_1, {link_number_1}, hit_position_1, {hit_normal_1}, ..., status_code]. A negative status_code indicates an error; otherwise, it represents the number of hits.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Ceil.documentation: Returns the smallest integer value greater than or equal to val (rounded toward positive infinity).

@sl-slua/global/ll.Char.documentation: Constructs and returns a single-character string representing the supplied Unicode value val.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ClearCameraParams.documentation: Resets all camera parameters to default values and turns off scripted camera control. Requires the PERMISSION_CONTROL_CAMERA runtime permission (automatically granted for attached or sat-on objects).

@sl-slua/global/ll.ClearLinkMedia.documentation: Deletes the media and clears all parameters from the given face on the linked prim. Returns an integer STATUS_* flag detailing the success or failure of the operation.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ClearPrimMedia.documentation: Deletes the media and clears all parameters from the specified face. Returns an integer STATUS_* flag detailing the success or failure of the operation.

@sl-slua/global/ll.CloseRemoteDataChanne.documentation: Deprecated. Closes the specified XML-RPC channel.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Cloud.documentation: Returns a float representing the cloud density at the prim's position offset by the vector offset.

@sl-slua/global/ll.CollisionFilter.documentation: Sets the collision filter, either exclusively or inclusively. If accept is TRUE, only collisions matching name and id are processed; if FALSE, matches are excluded. Pass an empty string or NULL_KEY to name or id to skip filtering on that parameter.

@sl-slua/global/ll.CollisionSound.documentation: Suppresses default collision sounds and replaces default impact sounds with impact_sound at the volume level specified by impact_volume. Supply an empty string to only suppress collision sounds.

@sl-slua/global/ll.CollisionSprite.documentation: Suppresses default collision sprites and replaces them with impact_sprite (which must be in the prim's inventory). Supply an empty string to only suppress collision sprites.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ComputeHash.documentation: Returns a hex-encoded hash digest string of message using the specified cryptographic algorithm.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Cos.documentation: Returns a float representing the cosine of theta.

@sl-slua/global/ll.CreateCharacter.documentation: Converts the linkset containing the script into a pathfinding character entity (required to use pathfinding functions) using the specified options.

@sl-slua/global/ll.CreateKeyValue.documentation: Starts an asynchronous transaction to create a key-value pair (k and v) associated with the script's experience. Returns a key query handle for the dataserver event. Fails with XP_ERROR_STORAGE_EXCEPTION if the key already exists.

@sl-slua/global/ll.CreateLink.documentation: Attempts to link the object containing the script with target. Requires the PERMISSION_CHANGE_LINKS runtime permission.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Damage.documentation: Generates a damage event delivering the specified amount of damage and damage_type to the targeted avatar or task in the same region.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DataSizeKeyValue.documentation: Starts an asynchronous transaction to request the used and total data storage allocated for the experience. Returns a key query handle for the dataserver event.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DeleteCharacter.documentation: Converts the linkset back to a standard physical object, removing all pathfinding properties.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DeleteKeyValue.documentation: Starts an asynchronous transaction to delete the key-value pair associated with key k in the experience. Returns a key query handle for the dataserver event.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DeleteSubList.documentation: Returns a copy of the list src with the slice from start to end (inclusive) removed. Negative indices count backward from the end of the list. If start is greater than end, the deletion excludes the specified range.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DeleteSubString.documentation: Returns a copy of the string src with the characters from start to end (inclusive) removed. Negative indices count backward from the end of the string. If start is greater than end, the deletion excludes the specified range.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DerezObject.documentation: Derezzes (deletes or returns) a targeted object in the region previously rezzed by a script in this linkset, returning TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetachFromAvatar.documentation: Detaches the object containing the script from the avatar. Requires the PERMISSION_ATTACH runtime permission (automatically granted to attached objects). Note that the detached object is completely removed from the region and not dropped on the ground.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedDamage.documentation: Returns a list containing pending damage information for the event specified by number, including the current damage, the damage type, and the original damage delivered.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedGrab.documentation: Returns a vector representing the grab offset of the user touching the object. Only works in touch events and returns <0.0, 0.0, 0.0> if number is not a valid index.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedGroup.documentation: Returns TRUE if the detected object or avatar specified by number has the same active group as the prim containing the script. Returns FALSE if the group is not active or if they are not in the group.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedKey.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the detected object or avatar specified by number, or NULL_KEY if number is not a valid index.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedLinkNumber.documentation: Returns the link number (integer) of the triggered event (touches and collisions only) specified by number. Returns 0 for non-linked objects, 1 for the root prim, and 2+ for child prims. Returns 0 if not supported by the event.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedName.documentation: Returns a string representing the name of the detected object or avatar specified by item. Returns an empty string if item is not a valid index.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedOwner.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the owner of the detected object specified by number. Returns an invalid key if number is not a valid index.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedPos.documentation: Returns the vector position (in region coordinates) of the detected object or avatar specified by number, or <0.0, 0.0, 0.0> if number is not a valid index.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedRezzer.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the object or avatar that rezzed the detected object specified by number.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedRot.documentation: Returns the rotation of the detected object or avatar specified by number, or <0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0> if number is not a valid offset index.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedTouchBinormal.documentation: Returns the surface binormal vector (tangent to the surface, pointing along the positive T (V) direction of tangent space) at the touched location specified by index. Can be used with llDetectedTouchNormal to determine the tangent space.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedTouchFace.documentation: Returns the integer index of the face clicked by the avatar in the touch event specified by index.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedTouchNormal.documentation: Returns the surface normal vector (perpendicular to the surface) at the touched location specified by index. Can be used with llDetectedTouchBinormal to determine the tangent space.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedTouchPos.documentation: Returns the vector position where the object was touched (specified by index) in region coordinates, or in screen-space coordinates if the object is attached as a HUD.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedTouchST.documentation: Returns the surface coordinates (<s, t, 0.0>) where the prim was touched, specified by index. X and Y contain the horizontal (s) and vertical (t) face coordinates, typically in the interval [0.0, 1.0]. Returns TOUCH_INVALID_TEXCOORD if coordinates cannot be determined.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedTouchUV.documentation: Returns the texture coordinates (<u, v, 0.0>) where the prim was touched, specified by index. X and Y contain the horizontal (u) and vertical (v) texture coordinates, typically in the interval [0.0, 1.0] (affected by repeats and rotation). Returns TOUCH_INVALID_TEXCOORD if coordinates cannot be determined.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedType.documentation: Returns an integer bitfield representing the types (AGENT, ACTIVE, PASSIVE, or SCRIPTED) of the detected object or avatar specified by number. Returns 0 if number is not a valid index.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DetectedVel.documentation: Returns the vector velocity of the detected object or avatar specified by number, or <0.0, 0.0, 0.0> if number is not a valid offset index.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Dialog.documentation: Shows a dialog box on the screen of the avatar specified by avatar, displaying message along with up to 12 choice buttons. Clicking a button chats its label on channel.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Die.documentation: Deletes the entire object containing the script (the object does not go to inventory). Use llBreakLink first to delete only a single prim.

@sl-slua/global/ll.DumpList2String.documentation: Returns a string that is the list src converted to a single string, with separator placed between each entry.

@sl-slua/global/ll.EdgeOfWorld.documentation: Checks if the border reached along the vector dir from the vector pos is the edge of the world (i.e., has no neighboring simulator). Returns TRUE if it hits the edge of the world, or FALSE if there is a neighboring simulator.

@sl-slua/global/ll.EjectFromLand.documentation: Ejects the specified avatar from land/parcels owned by the object's owner (group or resident).

@sl-slua/global/ll.Email.documentation: Sends an email to address with subject and message.

@sl-slua/global/ll.EscapeURL.documentation: Returns a string representing the escaped/encoded version of url, replacing spaces with '%20' and non-alphanumeric characters with their '%xx' hexadecimal UTF-8 equivalent.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Euler2Rot.documentation: Returns the rotation representation of the Euler angles vector v.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Evade.documentation: Directs a pathfinding character to evade target, attempting to hide from its pursuer if a hiding spot is available (i.e., no line of sight from the character's head to the pursuer's head, and no direct path on the navmesh).

@sl-slua/global/ll.ExecCharacterCmd.documentation: Sends a command (specified by command) to the pathing system with options. Currently only supports stopping pathfinding or making the character jump.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Fabs.documentation: Returns a float representing the absolute (positive) value of val.

@sl-slua/global/ll.FindNotecardTextCount.documentation: Searches the text of a cached notecard for lines containing the given pattern and returns the number of matches found through a dataserver event.

@sl-slua/global/ll.FindNotecardTextSync.documentation: Synchronously searches a cached notecard name for lines containing pattern, returning a list of line and column numbers. Returns a list containing 'NAK' if the notecard is not cached, or an empty list if no matches are found.

@sl-slua/global/ll.FleeFrom.documentation: Directs a pathfinding character to keep the specified distance from the target position vector (within the region or adjacent regions).

@sl-slua/global/ll.Floor.documentation: Returns the largest integer value less than or equal to val (rounded toward negative infinity).

@sl-slua/global/ll.ForceMouselook.documentation: Sets whether any avatar sitting on this prim is forced into mouselook mode. Setting mouselook to TRUE forces the mode; FALSE (default) allows the avatar to keep their current camera mode.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Frand.documentation: Returns a pseudo-random float in the range [0.0, mag) or (mag, 0.0] depending on the sign of mag. The value is inclusive of 0.0 but exclusive of mag.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GenerateKey.documentation: Generates and returns a unique versioned UUID key (utilizing SHA-1 hashing). Due to being versioned, it will not return NULL_KEY; however, the exact UUID version is an implementation detail that should not be relied upon.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetAccel.documentation: Returns a vector representing the acceleration of the object in the region's frame of reference.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetAgentInfo.documentation: Returns an integer bitfield containing status information about the agent specified by id (such as AGENT_FLYING, AGENT_ATTACHMENTS, AGENT_SITTING, etc.).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetAgentLanguage.documentation: Returns a string representing the language code of the preferred interface language set by the avatar.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetAgentList.documentation: Requests a list of avatar UUID keys for agents currently in the region, limited by scope. Returns a list of keys or a list containing an error message string.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetAgentSize.documentation: Returns a vector representing the estimated bounding box size of the specified avatar, or ZERO_VECTOR if they are not in the same region.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetAlpha.documentation: Returns a float representing the Blinn-Phong alpha (transparency) of face. If face is ALL_SIDES, returns the mean average of all faces.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetAnimation.documentation: Returns a string representing the name of the currently playing locomotion animation for the avatar specified by id.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetAnimationList.documentation: Returns a list of keys (UUIDs) representing all active animations currently playing on the specified avatar.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetAnimationOverride.documentation: Returns a string representing the name of the animation currently overriding the specified anim_state. Requires the PERMISSION_OVERRIDE_ANIMATIONS or PERMISSION_TRIGGER_ANIMATION runtime permission.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetAttached.documentation: Returns the integer attachment point (an ATTACH_* constant) that the object is attached to, or 0 if it is unattached or pending detachment.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetAttachedList.documentation: Returns a list of object keys (UUIDs) corresponding to public, visible (non-HUD) attachments worn by the specified avatar, in the order they were attached. Returns a list with an error message string on failure.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetAttachedListFilter.documentation: Retrieves a list of attachment object keys worn by avatar in the order they were attached, filtered by options. Returns a list containing an error message string on failure.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetBoundingBox.documentation: Returns the bounding box of object (including any linked prims) relative to its root prim in local coordinates, formatted as [ (vector) min_corner, (vector) max_corner ].

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetCameraAspect.documentation: Returns a float representing the camera's current aspect ratio (width/height) of the agent who granted PERMISSION_TRACK_CAMERA. Returns 0.0 if permissions are not granted.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetCameraFOV.documentation: Returns a float representing the camera's current field of view (FOV) in radians of the agent who granted PERMISSION_TRACK_CAMERA. Returns 0.0 if permissions are not granted.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetCameraPos.documentation: Returns a vector representing the camera's current position in region coordinates of the agent who granted PERMISSION_TRACK_CAMERA. Returns ZERO_VECTOR if permissions are not granted.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetCameraRot.documentation: Returns a rotation representing the camera's current orientation of the agent who granted PERMISSION_TRACK_CAMERA. Returns ZERO_ROTATION if permissions are not granted.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetCenterOfMass.documentation: Returns the vector position (in region coordinates) of the center of mass. Returns the individual child prim's center of mass if called from a child, or the entire linkset's center of mass if called from the root.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetClosestNavPoint.documentation: Returns a list containing the closest vector position on the navigation mesh (navmesh) to the specified point (expressed in region-local space), or an empty list if none is found. Configured using options.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetColor.documentation: Returns the Blinn-Phong RGB color vector of face (values between 0.0 and 1.0). If face is ALL_SIDES, returns the mean average of all faces.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetCreator.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the prim's original creator.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetDate.documentation: Returns a string representing the current date in the UTC time zone in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD'.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetDayLength.documentation: Returns an integer representing the number of seconds in the environmental day cycle applied to the current parcel.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetDayOffset.documentation: Returns an integer representing the offset duration (in seconds) added to calculate the current environmental time on this parcel.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetDisplayName.documentation: Returns the display name string of the avatar specified by id if they are in the region or cached; returns an empty string otherwise (use llRequestDisplayName if the avatar is absent).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetEnergy.documentation: Returns a float representing the remaining physics energy of the object as a percentage (0.0 to 1.0) of its maximum capacity.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetEnv.documentation: Returns a string containing the requested regional data specified by name.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetEnvironment.documentation: Returns a list containing the current environment parameters for the parcel or region at pos, retrieved in the order specified by params.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetExperienceDetails.documentation: Returns a list of details for the specified experience_id, formatted as [string experience_name, key owner_id, key experience_id, integer state, string state_message, key group_id].

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetExperienceErrorMes.documentation: Returns a text description of the specified experience error code, or a description of XP_ERROR_UNKNOWN_ERROR if the error is invalid.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetForce.documentation: Returns a vector representing the constant force currently applied to the object (if the object is physical).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetFreeMemory.documentation: Returns an integer representing the number of free bytes of memory currently available to the script.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetFreeURLs.documentation: Returns an integer representing the number of available HTTP URLs (remaining for the owner if the object is attached, or for the region if unattached).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetGMTclock.documentation: Returns a float representing the time in seconds since midnight GMT (truncated to whole seconds).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetGeometricCenter.documentation: Returns a vector representing the geometric center of the object relative to its root prim.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetHTTPHeader.documentation: Returns a string representing the value of the specified header associated with the HTTP request_id.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetHealth.documentation: Returns a float representing the current health of the avatar or object specified by id.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetInventoryAcquireTi.documentation: Returns a string containing the UTC timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ) representing when the inventory item was placed inside the prim.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetInventoryCreator.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the creator of the specified inventory item.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetInventoryDesc.documentation: Returns a string containing the description of the specified inventory item.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetInventoryKey.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the specified inventory name.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetInventoryName.documentation: Returns the name of the inventory item of the specified type (INVENTORY_*) at the index number.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetInventoryNumber.documentation: Returns an integer representing the quantity of items of the specified type (INVENTORY_*) in the prim's inventory.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetInventoryPermMask.documentation: Returns an integer bitfield representing the permission category mask of the specified inventory item.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetInventoryType.documentation: Returns an integer representing the INVENTORY_* type of the named inventory item.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetKey.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the prim containing the script.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetLandOwnerAt.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the land owner at the vector pos, or NULL_KEY if the land is public.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetLinkKey.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the linked prim or avatar specified by link.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetLinkMedia.documentation: Returns a list containing the media parameters of the specified face on the linked prim link, retrieved in the order requested by params.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetLinkName.documentation: Returns a string containing the name of the linked prim specified by link.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetLinkNumber.documentation: Returns the integer link number of the prim containing the script (0 for unlinked, 1 for the root, and 2+ for children).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetLinkNumberOfSides.documentation: Returns an integer representing the number of sides (faces) of the linked prim specified by link.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetLinkPrimitiveParam.documentation: Returns a list of primitive parameters requested in params for the linked prim specified by link (equivalent to llGetPrimitiveParams).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetLinkSitFlags.documentation: Returns an integer representing the active sit flags currently set on the linked prim specified by link.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetListEntryType.documentation: Returns an integer representing the variable type (TYPE_*) of the list entry at index in the list src.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetListLength.documentation: Returns an integer representing the total number of elements in the list src.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetLocalPos.documentation: Returns a position vector relative to the root prim. If called from the root prim, it returns the global region position (or the position relative to the attachment point if attached).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetLocalRot.documentation: Returns a rotation representing the local orientation of a child prim relative to the root prim, or the object's overall rotation if called from the root.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetMass.documentation: Returns a float representing the mass of the object (in lindograms). Returns the total linkset mass if called from the root, or the individual prim's mass if called from a child prim. Returns the wearer's mass if inside an attachment.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetMassMKS.documentation: Returns a float representing the mass of the object in kilograms. Functionally identical to llGetMass except for using MKS (metric) units.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetMaxScaleFactor.documentation: Returns a float representing the maximum scale factor that can be applied to the object via llScaleByFactor without violating size or linkability limits.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetMemoryLimit.documentation: Returns an integer representing the maximum memory limit (in bytes) that the script is allowed to allocate.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetMinScaleFactor.documentation: Returns a float representing the minimum scale factor that can be applied to the object via llScaleByFactor without violating size limits.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetMoonDirection.documentation: Returns a normalized vector representing the current direction of the parcel's moon, taking altitude into account. Falls back to the region's moon direction if no custom parcel environment is set.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetMoonRotation.documentation: Returns a rotation representing the orientation applied to the moon on the current parcel and altitude track. Falls back to the region's moon rotation if no custom parcel environment is set.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetNextEmail.documentation: Requests the next queued email matching the specified sender address and subject filters via the email event.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetNotecardLine.documentation: Asynchronously requests the line index line of the notecard name from the dataserver. Returns a key query handle for the dataserver event, which will return 'EOF' when reaching past the end of the notecard.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetNotecardLineSync.documentation: Synchronously reads the line index line of the notecard name from the region's cache, immediately returning its text without raising a dataserver event. Returns 'NAK' if not cached or 'EOF' if out of bounds.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetNumberOfNotecardLi.documentation: Asynchronously requests the total line count of the notecard name. Returns a key query handle for the dataserver event.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetNumberOfPrims.documentation: Returns an integer representing the total number of prims and seated avatars in the linkset containing the script.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetNumberOfSides.documentation: Returns an integer representing the total number of sides (faces) of the prim containing the script.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetObjectAnimationNam.documentation: Returns a list of strings representing the names of all active animations currently playing on the object.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetObjectDesc.documentation: Returns a string containing the description of the specific prim containing the script.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetObjectDetails.documentation: Returns a list containing the requested parameters of the specified avatar or object id, retrieved in the order requested by params.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetObjectLinkKey.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the linked child prim specified by link within the linkset identified by object_id.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetObjectMass.documentation: Returns a float representing the mass of the avatar or object specified by id.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetObjectName.documentation: Returns a string containing the name of the specific prim containing the script.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetObjectPermMask.documentation: Returns an integer bitfield representing the permission mask of the specified category for the object containing the script.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetObjectPrimCount.documentation: Returns an integer representing the total number of prims in the object containing the specified prim.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetOmega.documentation: Returns a vector representing the physical rotation (angular velocity) of the object in radians per second.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetOwner.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the object's current owner.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetOwnerKey.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the owner of the prim specified by id.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetParcelDetails.documentation: Returns a list containing the requested parcel details specified by params (retrieved in the same order) for the parcel at the vector pos.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetParcelFlags.documentation: Returns an integer bitfield of parcel flags (PARCEL_FLAG_*) for the parcel at the position pos.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetParcelMaxPrims.documentation: Returns an integer representing the maximum combined land impact (prim limit) allowed for objects on the parcel at pos, determined either for the single parcel or sim-wide based on sim_wide.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetParcelMusicURL.documentation: Returns a string containing the parcel's streaming music (audio) URL. The object owner must also be the land owner (or share the same group deeding).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetParcelPrimCount.documentation: Returns an integer representing the total land impact of objects on the parcel at pos in the specified category. If sim_wide is TRUE, returns combined usage for all regional parcels owned by the parcel owner; if FALSE, returns usage for only the specified parcel.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetParcelPrimOwners.documentation: Returns a list of up to 100 strides (formatted as [key owner, integer land_impact]) representing owners of objects on the parcel at pos, sorted by owner key. Requires owner-like permissions for the parcel and the script owner's presence in the region.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetPermissions.documentation: Returns an integer bitfield representing the permissions (PERMISSION_*) currently granted to the script.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetPermissionsKey.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) of the avatar that last granted or declined permissions to the script, or NULL_KEY if the permissions request was ignored or cancelled.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetPhysicsMaterial.documentation: Returns a list of the format [float gravity_multiplier, float restitution, float friction, float density] detailing the physical characteristics of the object.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetPos.documentation: Returns a vector representing the current position of the object in region coordinates.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetPrimMediaParams.documentation: Returns a list containing the media parameters of the specified face, retrieved in the order requested by params. Returns an empty list if no media exists on the face.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetPrimitiveParams.documentation: Returns a list of primitive attribute values matching the requested params list.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionAgentCount.documentation: Returns an integer representing the current number of avatars in the region.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionCorner.documentation: Returns a vector (in meters) representing the global grid coordinates of the south-west corner of the current region (Z component is always 0.0).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionDayLength.documentation: Returns an integer representing the total number of seconds in the region-wide day cycle.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionDayOffset.documentation: Returns an integer representing the offset duration (in seconds) added to calculate the current environmental time for the region.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionFPS.documentation: Returns a float representing the average region simulator frames per second (FPS).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionFlags.documentation: Returns an integer bitfield representing the region flags (REGION_FLAG_*) currently enabled for the region containing the object.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionMoonDirectio.documentation: Returns a normalized vector representing the current direction of the region's moon, taking altitude into account.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionMoonRotation.documentation: Returns a rotation representing the orientation applied to the moon at the region level, taking altitude track into account.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionName.documentation: Returns a string containing the name of the current region.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionSunDirection.documentation: Returns a normalized vector representing the current direction of the region's sun, taking altitude into account.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionSunRotation.documentation: Returns a rotation representing the orientation applied to the sun at the region level, taking altitude track into account.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionTimeDilation.documentation: Returns a float representing the current physics time dilation of the region, ranging from 0.0 (full dilation / slow) to 1.0 (no dilation / real-world speed).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRegionTimeOfDay.documentation: Returns a float with subsecond precision representing the elapsed seconds since region environmental midnight or region uptime (whichever is smaller). If the region's sun position is fixed, returns region uptime.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRenderMaterial.documentation: Returns a string representing the render material on face (returns the inventory name if it is in the prim's inventory, or its key UUID otherwise).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRootPosition.documentation: Returns a vector representing the position (in region coordinates) of the root prim of the linkset containing the script.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRootRotation.documentation: Returns a rotation representing the orientation (relative to the region) of the root prim of the linkset containing the script.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetRot.documentation: Returns a rotation representing the prim's orientation relative to the region's axes.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetSPMaxMemory.documentation: Returns an integer representing the maximum memory (in bytes) used by the script while the memory profiler was last active (only valid after using PROFILE_SCRIPT_MEMORY).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetScale.documentation: Returns a vector representing the physical scale (dimensions in meters) of the prim containing the script.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetScriptName.documentation: Returns a string containing the name of the script calling this function.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetScriptState.documentation: Returns a boolean integer indicating whether the specified script in the prim's inventory is running (returns TRUE if running, FALSE otherwise).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetSimStats.documentation: Returns a float containing the value of the requested region statistic specified by stat_type.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetSimulatorHostname.documentation: Returns a string containing the hostname of the server machine running the script (e.g., 'sim225.agni.lindenlab.com').

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetStartParameter.documentation: Returns an integer representing the start/rez parameter passed to the object on creation (returns 0 if rezzed by an agent).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetStartString.documentation: Returns the initialization string passed to the object's root prim on rez with llRezObjectWithParams (via REZ_PARAM_STRING; returns an empty string if rezzed by an agent).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetStaticPath.documentation: Returns a list of position vectors representing pathfinding waypoints between start and end on the static navmesh for a character of the specified radius. Ignores movable obstacles and can be used in any region regardless of dynamic pathfinding status.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetStatus.documentation: Returns a boolean integer indicating whether the specified status flag status is enabled for the object.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetSubString.documentation: Returns a copy of the substring from src within the inclusive range specified by the start and end indices. Negative indices count backward from the end. If start is greater than end, the substring is the excluded range.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetSunDirection.documentation: Returns a normalized vector representing the current direction of the parcel's sun, taking altitude into account. Falls back to the region's sun direction if no custom parcel environment is set.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetSunRotation.documentation: Returns a rotation representing the orientation applied to the sun on the current parcel and altitude track. Falls back to the region's sun rotation if no custom parcel environment is set.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetTexture.documentation: Returns a string representing the Blinn-Phong diffuse texture on face (returns the inventory name if it is a texture in the prim's inventory, or its key UUID otherwise).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetTextureOffset.documentation: Returns a vector containing the texture offsets of face in the X (horizontal U) and Y (vertical V) components (Z is unused).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetTextureRot.documentation: Returns a float representing the texture rotation angle (in radians) on face.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetTextureScale.documentation: Returns a vector containing the texture scales of face in the X and Y components (Z is unused).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetTime.documentation: Returns a float representing the elapsed script time in seconds with subsecond precision (since the script started, was reset, or since the last call to llResetTime or llGetAndResetTime).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetTimeOfDay.documentation: Returns a float with subsecond precision representing the elapsed seconds since parcel environmental midnight or region uptime (whichever is smaller). If the parcel's sun position is fixed, returns region uptime.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetTimestamp.documentation: Returns a string containing the current date and time in the UTC time zone formatted as an ISO 8601 timestamp ('YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.ff..fZ').

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetTorque.documentation: Returns a vector representing the physical torque force currently acting on the object (if the object is physical).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetUnixTime.documentation: Returns an integer representing the current Unix timestamp (the number of seconds elapsed since 00:00:00 Jan 1, 1970 UTC).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetUsedMemory.documentation: Returns an integer representing the total number of bytes of memory currently used by the script (non-Mono scripts always return 16,384 bytes).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetUsername.documentation: Returns a string representing the unique username of the avatar specified by id if they are connected to the region or cached; returns an empty string otherwise (use llRequestUsername if the avatar is absent).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetVel.documentation: Returns a vector representing the velocity of the object (in meters per second) relative to the global region coordinates. For physical objects, returns the velocity of its center of mass.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetVisualParams.documentation: Returns a list containing the values of the visual parameters requested in params for the agent specified by agentid.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GetWallclock.documentation: Returns a float representing the time in seconds since midnight Pacific Time (PST/PDT), which is equivalent to Second Life Time (SLT) truncated to whole seconds.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GiveAgentInventory.documentation: Gives the specified inventory items to the agent as a new folder named folder, as permitted by the permissions system. Customizes the transfer using options.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GiveInventory.documentation: Gives the specified inventory item to the destination, as permitted by the permissions system. The destination can be any agent, or an object located in the same region.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GiveInventoryList.documentation: Gives the list of inventory items to target as a new folder named folder. If target is an object, the items are passed directly into its inventory and no folder is created. The target must be an agent or an object in the same region.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GiveMoney.documentation: Transfers the specified amount of L$ from the script owner to the destination avatar. Silently fails if the PERMISSION_DEBIT permission has not been granted. Returns 0 (use llTransferLindenDollars to match transactions to transaction_result events).

@sl-slua/global/ll.GodLikeRezObject.documentation: Rezzes an object directly from a UUID specified by inventory at the position pos, provided the owner has the god-bit set.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Ground.documentation: Returns a float representing the ground height directly below the prim's position offset by the vector offset.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GroundContour.documentation: Returns a vector representing the ground contour direction (the direction with no change in elevation) directly below the prim's position offset by the vector offset.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GroundNormal.documentation: Returns a vector representing the ground surface normal vector directly below the current position offset by the vector offset.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GroundRepel.documentation: Critically damps the object's vertical motion to height (using critical damping timescale tau) if it is within height * 0.5 of the terrain (or water level if water is TRUE). Only works on physics-enabled objects; do not use with vehicles.

@sl-slua/global/ll.GroundSlope.documentation: Returns a vector representing the ground slope directly below the prim's position offset by the vector offset.

@sl-slua/global/ll.HMAC.documentation: Returns a Base64-encoded HMAC hash of msg using the secret key private_key and the specified digest algorithm (md5, sha1, sha224, sha256, sha384, or sha512).

@sl-slua/global/ll.HTTPRequest.documentation: Sends an HTTP request to the specified url containing body and configured via parameters. Raises an http_response event and returns a key query handle identifying the request.

@sl-slua/global/ll.HTTPResponse.documentation: Responds to the incoming HTTP request identified by request_id with the HTTP status code status and the payload body.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Hash.documentation: Returns an integer representing the 32-bit hash value of the string val (returns 0 if the string is empty).

@sl-slua/global/ll.InsertString.documentation: Returns a new string representing dst with src inserted starting at the zero-based index pos. Note that this operation does not modify dst itself.

@sl-slua/global/ll.InstantMessage.documentation: Sends an instant message containing message to the user identified by their key.

@sl-slua/global/ll.IntegerToBase64.documentation: Returns an 8-character Base64 string representing the big-endian encoded value of number.

@sl-slua/global/ll.IsFriend.documentation: Returns TRUE if agent_id and the owner of the script are friends, and FALSE otherwise.

@sl-slua/global/ll.IsLinkGLTFMaterial.documentation: Checks the specified face on the linked prim link. Returns TRUE if the face material is a PBR render material, or FALSE if it uses Blinn-Phong diffuse textures.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Json2List.documentation: Parses the JSON string src and returns a list representing its top-level elements.

@sl-slua/global/ll.JsonGetValue.documentation: Parses the JSON string json and returns the value found by traversing the specified path of specifiers as a string.

@sl-slua/global/ll.JsonSetValue.documentation: Returns a new JSON string representing json with the target located at specifiers set to value. Supports JSON_APPEND to append elements. Writing JSON_DELETE deletes the target. Overwriting array bounds or setting non-array levels with array indices returns JSON_INVALID.

@sl-slua/global/ll.JsonValueType.documentation: Parses the JSON string json and returns the JSON type constant (JSON_*) representing the value found at specifiers.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Key2Name.documentation: Returns a string containing the name of the prim or avatar specified by id. The target must be a valid, rezzed entity in the current region, otherwise an empty string is returned. Avatars return their legacy name.

@sl-slua/global/ll.KeyCountKeyValue.documentation: Starts an asynchronous transaction requesting the total count of keys in the experience data store. Returns a key query handle for the dataserver event.

@sl-slua/global/ll.KeysKeyValue.documentation: Starts an asynchronous transaction to retrieve count keys from the experience data store starting at the zero-based index first. Returns a key query handle for the dataserver event. Fails with XP_ERROR_KEY_NOT_FOUND if out of bounds.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Linear2sRGB.documentation: Returns a vector representing the conversion of color from the linear RGB colorspace into the sRGB colorspace.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinkAdjustSoundVolume.documentation: Adjusts the volume of the currently playing sound attached to the linked prim link (has no effect on sounds started with llTriggerSound).

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinkParticleSystem.documentation: Creates or updates a particle system on the linked prim link based on the list of rules. An empty list removes the particle system.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinkPlaySound.documentation: Plays the specified sound on the linked prim link (once or looping) at volume. Only one sound can be attached to a prim at a time; new attachments or calling llStopSound stops previous playback. Controlled by flags.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinkSetSoundQueueing.documentation: Enables or disables sound queuing on the linked prim link. When set to TRUE, sounds will queue and play in sequence.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinkSetSoundRadius.documentation: Limits the audibility radius of attached and triggered scripted sounds to distance radius (in meters) around the linked prim link.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinkSitTarget.documentation: Sets the sit target position (offset) and orientation (rot) for the linked prim link, relative to the prim's own position and rotation. Clear by setting offset to <0.0, 0.0, 0.0>.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinkStopSound.documentation: Stops the playback of any currently playing attached sound on the linked prim link.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataAvailable.documentation: Returns an integer representing the number of bytes available/remaining in the linkset's datastore.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataCountFound.documentation: Returns the total count of keys in the linkset datastore that match the regular expression pattern.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataCountKeys.documentation: Returns an integer representing the total number of unique keys stored in the linkset's datastore.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataDelete.documentation: Deletes the unprotected key-value pair specified by name from the linkset's datastore, triggering a linkset_data event.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataDeleteFoun.documentation: Deletes all keys in the datastore matching the regular expression pattern. Returns a list [num_deleted, num_failed_protected]. Decrypts and deletes protected keys if matching pass is provided.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataDeleteProt.documentation: Deletes the protected key-value pair specified by name from the linkset's datastore using the passphrase pass, triggering a linkset_data event.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataFindKeys.documentation: Returns an alphabetically sorted list of up to count keys from the datastore that match the regular expression pattern, starting at index start (returns all matching keys if count < 1).

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataListKeys.documentation: Returns an alphabetically sorted list of up to count keys from the datastore, starting at index start (returns all keys if count < 1).

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataRead.documentation: Reads and returns the string value corresponding to key name from the linkset's datastore.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataReadProtec.documentation: Reads and returns the string value of the protected key name from the linkset's datastore using the passphrase pass.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataReset.documentation: Erases all key-value pairs stored in the linkset's datastore, triggering a linkset_data event (with LINKSETDATA_RESET) in all scripts in the linkset.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataWrite.documentation: Creates or updates an unprotected key-value pair (name and value) in the linkset's datastore. Returns an integer success or failure code.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LinksetDataWriteProte.documentation: Creates or updates a protected key-value pair (name and value) in the linkset's datastore using the passphrase pass. Returns an integer success or failure code.

@sl-slua/global/ll.List2CSV.documentation: Returns a string of comma-separated values taken in order from the list src.

@sl-slua/global/ll.List2Float.documentation: Returns the float value at index in the list src. Returns 0.0 if the index is out of bounds or if the value cannot be type-cast.

@sl-slua/global/ll.List2Integer.documentation: Returns the integer value at index in the list src. Returns 0 if the index is out of bounds or if the value cannot be type-cast.

@sl-slua/global/ll.List2Json.documentation: Converts the list values into a JSON string of the specified type (either a JSON_ARRAY or a JSON_OBJECT). Returns JSON_INVALID if an error is encountered.

@sl-slua/global/ll.List2Key.documentation: Returns the key (UUID) value at index in the list src. Returns a null string/key if the index is out of bounds or if the value cannot be type-cast.

@sl-slua/global/ll.List2List.documentation: Returns a new list containing the subset of entries from src within the inclusive range specified by the start and end indices. Negative indices count backward from the end.

@sl-slua/global/ll.List2ListSlice.documentation: Returns a list containing the slice_index'th element of every stride within the inclusive range from start to end in the strided list src. Stride must be a positive integer.

@sl-slua/global/ll.List2ListStrided.documentation: Returns a new list containing the first element of every stride in the strided list src within the inclusive range from start to end.

@sl-slua/global/ll.List2Rot.documentation: Returns the rotation value at index in the list src. Returns ZERO_ROTATION if the index is out of bounds or if the value cannot be type-cast.

@sl-slua/global/ll.List2String.documentation: Returns the string value at index in the list src. Returns an empty string if the index is out of bounds.

@sl-slua/global/ll.List2Vector.documentation: Returns the vector value at index in the list src. Returns ZERO_VECTOR if the index is out of bounds or if the value cannot be type-cast.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ListFindList.documentation: Returns the integer index of the first instance of list test within the list src (returns nil if not found).

@sl-slua/global/ll.ListFindListNext.documentation: Returns the integer index of the specified instance of list test within the list src (returns nil if not found).

@sl-slua/global/ll.ListFindStrided.documentation: Returns the integer index of the first instance of list test in the strided list src within the range from start to end (stepping through by stride). Returns nil if not found.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ListInsertList.documentation: Returns a new list containing all elements of dest with the elements of src inserted starting at index start. Does not modify dest itself.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ListRandomize.documentation: Returns a randomized copy of the list src by blocks of size stride. If the list length is not perfectly divisible by stride, no randomization occurs.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ListReplaceList.documentation: Returns a copy of the list dest with the inclusive range from start to end removed, and the elements of src inserted in its place at start.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ListSort.documentation: Returns a copy of the list src, sorted into blocks of stride in ascending order (if ascending is TRUE) or descending order (if FALSE). Only works if the first entry of each block shares the same datatype.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ListSortStrided.documentation: Returns a copy of the list src sorted into blocks of stride by the element at stride_index in each block. Sorted in ascending order (if ascending is TRUE) or descending order (if FALSE).

@sl-slua/global/ll.ListStatistics.documentation: Returns a float representing the result of performing the statistical aggregate function operation (a LIST_STAT_* constant) on the numeric list src.

@sl-slua/global/ll.Listen.documentation: Creates a listener on channel from name and id for msg. Returns an integer listener handle used to control or remove the listener. Empty strings or NULL_KEY filters do not filter on those parameters.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ListenControl.documentation: Enables or disables the listener specified by the integer handle. If active is TRUE, the listener is activated; if FALSE, it is deactivated.

@sl-slua/global/ll.ListenRemove.documentation: Completely removes the listener specified by the integer handle.

@sl-slua/global/ll.LoadURL.documentation: Shows a dialog box displaying message to the avatar avatar offering to open the specified url. Clicking yes launches the URL in their default web browser.
